prompt: From the support email below, extract a JSON object with EXACTLY these keys: customer_name (string), account_id (string), issue_category (one of: billing, outage, feature_request, bug), severity (one of: low, medium, high, critical), affected_since (ISO date YYYY-MM-DD or null if not stated), requested_refund (boolean). Output ONLY the JSON object, no commentary. Do not invent values that are not supported by the email; use null where the email is silent (except booleans, which default to false).

inputs.name: ticket.txt
inputs.content:
From: Priya Menon <priya@northwind.example>
Subject: URGENT - dashboards down since Tuesday

Hi, this is Priya from Northwind (account NW-4821). Our analytics dashboards have been completely unavailable since Tuesday the 8th of July and it is blocking our month-end close. This is now business-critical for us. I'm not asking for money back at this stage, I just need it fixed today. Please escalate.

Thanks,
Priya

grading.guidance: Gold JSON: {"customer_name":"Priya Menon","account_id":"NW-4821","issue_category":"outage","severity":"critical","affected_since":"2025-07-08","requested_refund":false}. (Year 2025 is the reasonable inference from context; accept 2025-07-08; 'business-critical'/'URGENT' => critical; explicitly not asking for refund => false.) A 10 matches all six fields with correct types and the exact enum values, uses false for refund, and resolves 'Tuesday the 8th of July' to 2025-07-08. Penalize any hallucinated refund=true, wrong category (e.g. bug), severity below high, or inventing an account/date not in the text. Slightly penalize extra keys or prose outside the JSON.
